Description: A small, idiomatic, STANDARD-LIBRARY-ONLY JSON HTTP endpoint. No external dependencies — use encoding/json and net/http only. All files use package main.

Project: numkit
Language: go
Description: A tiny, idiomatic, stdlib-only Go package named "numkit" with two pure integer functions and table-driven tests. No external dependencies, no unicode. All files use package numkit. Ground-truth values are plain arithmetic the tests can state exactly.
Go module path: guildlm.dev/numkit

TARGET_FILE: numkit.go
Purpose of this file: package numkit. GCD(a, b int) int returns the greatest common divisor of the absolute values of a and b using the Euclidean algorithm; GCD(0,0) is 0. Clamp(x, lo, hi int) int returns x bounded to the inclusive range [lo, hi]; if lo > hi the function returns x unchanged.

All files in this project:
  - go.mod: Go module file. Module path guildlm.dev/numkit, go 1.23. No dependencies. Output as a ```mod block, not Go.
  - numkit.go: package numkit. GCD(a, b int) int returns the greatest common divisor of the absolute values of a and b using the Euclidean algorithm; GCD(0,0) is 0. Clamp(x, lo, hi int) int returns x bounded to the inclusive range [lo, hi]; if lo > hi the function returns x unchanged.
  - numkit_test.go: package numkit. Table-driven tests for GCD and Clamp covering: GCD of coprime numbers (GCD(8,15)=1), a common factor (GCD(12,18)=6), a zero operand (GCD(0,5)=5), and negatives (GCD(-12,18)=6); Clamp below range (Clamp(-1,0,10)=0), inside range (Clamp(5,0,10)=5), above range (Clamp(99,0,10)=10), and inverted bounds returning x unchanged (Clamp(7,10,0)=7).

Project: taskflow
Language: go
Description: A larger, realistic, STANDARD-LIBRARY-ONLY HTTP backend for a task tracker — the kind of multi-resource, layered Go service that stresses cross-file consistency. No external dependencies whatsoever: net/http + ServeMux for routing (never a third-party router), encoding/json for bodies, sync for concurrency. All files use package main. Layers: sentinel errors, domain models with validation, a generic-ish in-memory repository, HTTP middleware, JSON + pagination helpers, two resource handlers (tasks and projects), a router that wires everything, and a main with graceful shutdown.
Go module path: guildlm.dev/taskflow

TARGET_FILE: errors.go
Purpose of this file: package main. Shared sentinel errors used across the store and handlers, all matchable with errors.Is: ErrNotFound, ErrExists, ErrValidation. Standard library only (errors).

All files in this project:
  - go.mod: Go module file. Module path guildlm.dev/taskflow, go 1.23. No dependencies. Output as a ```mod block, not Go.
  - errors.go: package main. Shared sentinel errors used across the store and handlers, all matchable with errors.Is: ErrNotFound, ErrExists, ErrValidation. Standard library only (errors).
  - models.go: package main. Domain models. A Task struct {ID string, Title string, Status string, ProjectID string} and a Project struct {ID string, Name string}. Valid Task.Status values are "todo", "doing", "done". A method (Task) Validate() error that returns a wrapped ErrValidation (use fmt.Errorf with %w) when Title is empty or Status is not one of the three allowed values; a (Project) Validate() error that returns ErrValidation when Name is empty. Standard library only.
  - store.go: package main. In-memory repository for BOTH resources. A Store struct holding two maps (tasks map[string]Task, projects map[string]Project) guarded by a single sync.RWMutex, built by NewStore(). Methods: CreateTask(Task) error / GetTask(id) (Task, error) / ListTasks() []Task / DeleteTask(id) error and the same four for Project. Create returns ErrExists on duplicate ID; Get/Delete return ErrNotFound. List methods return items sorted by ID. Standard library only (sync, sort, errors as needed).
  - middleware.go: package main. A Middleware type func(http.Handler) http.Handler and Chain(h, mws...) where the first listed is outermost. A Logging middleware (method, path, resolved status via a statusRecorder wrapping http.ResponseWriter defaulting to 200, and latency) and a Recover middleware (downstream panic -> 500). Standard library only.
  - httpjson.go: package main. HTTP/JSON helpers reused by every handler: writeJSON(w http.ResponseWriter, status int, v any) that sets Content-Type application/json and encodes v; writeError(w, status int, msg string) that writes a JSON {"error": msg}; and decodeJSON(r *http.Request, dst any) error that decodes the request body and returns an error on malformed input. Standard library only (encoding/json, net/http).
  - pagination.go: package main. func parsePage(r *http.Request) (limit, offset int) that reads ?limit= and ?offset= query params with strconv, defaulting limit to 20 (max 100) and offset to 0, clamping negatives to the default/zero. A GENERIC func paginate[T any](items []T, limit, offset int) []T that safely slices with bounds checks (offset past the end returns empty, never panics) — used for BOTH tasks and projects. Standard library only (net/http, strconv).
  - tasks_handler.go: package main. A TaskHandler struct holding *Store, built by NewTaskHandler(*Store). http.HandlerFunc methods using the httpjson + pagination helpers and r.PathValue("id"): Create (decodeJSON a Task, call Task.Validate returning 400 with writeError on ErrValidation via errors.Is, store it returning 201, 409 on ErrExists); List (parsePage then paginate(tasks, limit, offset), 200 with the page as JSON, optional ?status= filter); Get (200 or 404); Delete (204 or 404). Standard library only.
  - projects_handler.go: package main. A ProjectHandler struct holding *Store, built by NewProjectHandler(*Store). http.HandlerFunc methods mirroring the task handler for projects: Create (validate -> 400, store -> 201, 409), List (200 JSON array), Get (200/404), Delete (204/404). Reuse the httpjson helpers. Standard library only.
  - router.go: package main. func NewRouter(s *Store) http.Handler building an http.ServeMux (NOT a third-party router) wiring both handlers: POST/GET /tasks, GET/DELETE /tasks/{id}, POST/GET /projects, GET/DELETE /projects/{id}, and GET /health -> 200 "ok". Wrap with Chain(mux, Logging, Recover) and return it so tests can drive the fully-wired handler. Standard library only.
  - main.go: package main. func main() builds NewStore + NewRouter, runs an *http.Server on :8080 with graceful shutdown via signal.NotifyContext(context.Background(), os.Interrupt) and srv.Shutdown under a short context.WithTimeout. Standard library only.
  - taskflow_test.go: package main. Isolated table/flow tests (net/http/httptest) against NewRouter(NewStore()). Each case builds a FRESH router+store (never share state) and a FRESH request body per request (never reuse a drained buffer). Cover: create a task -> 201 and echo; invalid task (bad status) -> 400; duplicate task ID -> 409; GET missing -> 404; list tasks -> 200 with a JSON array; pagination (?limit=1) returns at most 1; delete -> 204 then GET -> 404; malformed JSON (truncated bytes, not empty {}) -> 400; and the same create/get happy-path for a project. Standard library only.

Description: A larger, realistic, STANDARD-LIBRARY-ONLY HTTP backend for a task tracker — the kind of multi-resource, layered Go service that stresses cross-file consistency. No external dependencies whatsoever: net/http + ServeMux for routing (never a third-party router), encoding/json for bodies, sync for concurrency. All files use package main. Layers: sentinel errors, domain models with validation, a generic-ish in-memory repository, HTTP middleware, JSON + pagination helpers, two resource handlers (tasks and projects), a router that wires everything, and a main with graceful shutdown.
Go module path: guildlm.dev/taskflow

TARGET_FILE: models.go
Purpose of this file: package main. Domain models. A Task struct {ID string, Title string, Status string, ProjectID string} and a Project struct {ID string, Name string}. Valid Task.Status values are "todo", "doing", "done". A method (Task) Validate() error that returns a wrapped ErrValidation (use fmt.Errorf with %w) when Title is empty or Status is not one of the three allowed values; a (Project) Validate() error that returns ErrValidation when Name is empty. Standard library only.

package main

import (
	"net/http"
)

// NewRouter builds an http.ServeMux mapping method+pattern routes to a UserHandler.
func NewRouter(s Store) http.Handler {
	mux := http.NewServeMux()
	userHandler := NewUserHandler(s)

	mux.HandleFunc("POST /users", userHandler.Create)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /users", userHandler.List)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /users/{id}", userHandler.Get)
	mux.HandleFunc("DELETE /users/{id}", userHandler.Delete)
	mux.HandleFunc("GET /health", func(w http.ResponseWriter, r *http.Request) {
		_, _ = w.Write([]byte("ok"))
	})

	return Chain(mux, Logging, Recover)
}
